entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager Routes and Stops with FOW
entry.content:
Dear Experts!

 

I would like to ask some questions about Field Operation Worker functionalities and the maps in general.

 

1 - Field operation worker functionality add-on component is available for use in SAP ECC?

2 - When activated, i understand that all my work orders correspond to Routes and the operation of the orders that has equipment is Stops. If i have more then one operation with other equipments i will have more than one Stop. I understand that if i go to my map and want to see the directions of this Work order, it will appear the direction for stop 1, stop 2 and so on. Is that correct? 

3 - The route details displays the ID, Description, Priority, Order Type, Mobile Status ,Due Date, Number of stops, all of this is related to the order, is that correct?

4 - Is it possible to print the Route overview page? I could't find any videos or photos related to this part for me to visualize better.

5 - SAM Demo is unavailable the route and stop functionality?

 

That's a question for SAM Maps in general.

6 - Is there a way to represent linear assets in SAM?

7 - Related to SAM - GIS integration. There is a limitation in which Equipment and functional location created in SAP before without coordinates, to include them in the Map, it is necessary to take the coordinates in GIS and manually include in the table /smfnd/oid01_gs or by program these coordinate values of the equipment to appear in sequence in SAM. Is that correct? If not could you explain in more details this SAM - GIS integration please?

 

Thank you!

Rafael Eij Umeda

SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Re: In Correct Page Numbers in Stitching PDF
entry.content: gladsonjacob - Could you please explain a bit more on how the above requirement was acheived.

entry.title: Budget hold back in Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to have a hold back amount in Variable Pay similar to the functionality mentioned here? https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2084496 

The budget based on planner is not available for Variable Pay. I am checking if this is possible with standard budget configuration. I know it can be achieved by budget on custom field. Thanks in advance.

@xavierlegarrec

entry.title: SAP Ariba User Provisioning Using SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS)
entry.content:
This blog post explains how to provision users to SAP Ariba using the SAP Cloud Identity Services—Identity Provisioning Service (IPS). Typically, you can create and provision new users to SAP Ariba through various methods such as: 

  • Self-registration
  • CSV file upload
  • Provisioning via IPS from existing user stores (e.g., Microsoft AD, AS ABAP, SAP SuccessFactors)

More information about Integrating SAP Ariba with SAP Cloud Identity Services can be found on SAP Help. While there are multiple options, this post focuses on using SAP Identity Provisioning to provision users from SAP Cloud Identity Service (Identity Authentication).

Note: For guidance on integrating Cloud Identity Services with SAP Ariba (for Single Sign-On and identity federation), see the blog post here:
Identity Federation & SAP Ariba SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services

Use-Case Scenario

Integrating Cloud Identity Services with SAP Ariba is just one example of how enterprises can unify identity management across various SAP solutions (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Business Network, SAP Category Management, SAP Fieldglass). Centralizing identities in SAP Cloud Identity Service is an essential part of an enterprise-wide identity strategy. It also aligns with future SAP initiatives such as SAP Task Center and Joule, which rely on users being managed in Cloud Identity Service.

Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning play a critical role in integrating SAP Ariba and other SAP BTP applications. They enable the use of a Global User ID—an attribute that uniquely identifies a user across the system landscape—serving as a prerequisite for adopting tools like SAP Task Center.

Architecture Overview

The architecture diagram for this scenario primarily highlights how to provision identities to SAP Ariba via Identity Provisioning. Similar concepts can also apply to other SAP Ariba SaaS solutions such as SAP Business Network, SAP Category Management, and SAP Fieldglass.

In this blog, we will cover:

  1. Creating Ariba users in SAP Cloud Identity Service using both ODM and a custom Ariba schema.
  2. Setting up SAP Ariba as the target system and Identity Authentication as the source system.
  3. Configuring the IPS transformation file to map Identity Authentication user attributes to SAP Ariba user attributes.
  4. Configuring IPS jobs to provision users to SAP Ariba.
  5. Planning the migration from the custom Ariba Cloud Identity Service schema to the standard SAP Ariba schema (once released).

Create Ariba Users in SAP Cloud Identity Service Using ODM and a Custom Ariba Schema

Beyond the standard/core user attributes in SAP Cloud Identity Service, SAP Ariba requires additional attributes (such as purchasing unit, purchasing organization, plant, etc.). To handle these, two extension schemas come into play:

  • ODM Schema: urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap.odm:2.0:User
  • SAP Ariba Schema: urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:ariba:2.0:User

SAP Cloud Identity Service has recently released Open Domain Model (ODM) schema support, enabling additional business attributes (e.g., companyCode, purchasingOrganization) in the Cloud Identity Service user profile. The ODM schema and attributes can be found under Users & Authentication → Schemas in your Cloud Identity Service.

Note: As of this writing, the SAP Ariba Schema (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:ariba:2.0:User) is planned for release in Q2 2025. In the interim, a custom schema must be created, which you will later migrate to the standard SAP Ariba schema once it becomes available.

Steps to Create a Custom SAP Ariba Schema

You can create the custom schema either via Postman or directly in the Cloud Identity Service interface. Below are the steps for creating it through the Cloud Identity Service UI:

  1. Navigate to Schemas
    • In the Cloud Identity Service console, go to Users & Authorizations → Schemas.
  2. Create New Schema
    • Click Create or Add, and enter a name for your custom schema. 
    • Important: The custom schema name (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:vendor:ariba:2.0:User) must be different from the future standard schema name (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:ariba:2.0:User) to avoid conflicts once the standard schema is released. 
  3. Define Schema Attributes
    • Add the additional attributes (e.g., currency, deliverTo, passwordAdapter, addresses). 

Create an Ariba User Using ODM and the Custom Ariba Schema in Cloud Identity Service

  1. Create a System User
    • In the Cloud Identity Service console, go to Users & Authorizations → Administrators → Add System.
    • Generate the Client ID and Client Secret, ensuring you have Manage Users permissions.

2. Prepare a User Payload in Postman

    • Use a sample JSON payload that includes ODM attributes and the custom Ariba attributes.
    • Ensure any attributes values you include must match valid SAP Ariba fields values so that the data correctly populates in SAP Ariba

3. Verify User Creation

    • After sending the request to create a user, check the Cloud Identity Service to confirm the new user.
    • The Extensions tab in the user record should display both ODM and custom Ariba attributes.

4. Assign the User to a Group

    • In Cloud Identity Service, assign the user to any necessary group(s) (for ex: ARIBA-USERS group) to manage their access permissions.

       

This completes the initial step of creating an Ariba user in Cloud Identity Service with both ODM and custom Ariba schema attributes.

Set Up Identity Authentication as the Source System and SAP Ariba as the Target System in IPS

Identity Authentication as the Source System

  1. Navigate to Identity Provisioning in your Cloud Identity Service console.
  2. Create a Source System and select Identity Authentication as the source type.
  3. Modify default transformation file to map ODM and custom Ariba schema attributes.
    • Download sample source transformation file from Github .
    • Note that the schema references in the transformation file should point to the ODM attributes and your custom Ariba schema attributes.

       

    • In the Source System Properties, you can set filters like ias.group.filter or ias.user.filter so that only specific users (e.g., aribacisuser1) are provisioned. 

SAP Ariba as the Target System

  1. In Identity Provisioning, navigate to Target Systems and create a new system of type SAP Ariba Application.

     

  2. SAP Ariba user management is performed via SCIM APIs. Follow the SAP Help documentation to request access to the SAP Ariba SCIM API, after which you will receive OAuth client credentials.
  3. Download and adjust the Target System Transformation File
    • Map your custom Ariba schema attributes to the standard SAP Ariba attributes.
    • For instance, ODM schema attributes can map to Accounting attributes in Ariba, while custom schema attributes might map to Shipping and Billing details.

Configure IPS Jobs to Provision Users to SAP Ariba

Once your source and target systems are set up:

  1. Run the Provisioning Job in IPS.
  2. The user you filtered (e.g., aribacisuser1) should be created in the SAP Ariba target system.
  3. In the Ariba system, navigate to Manage → Core Administration → User Manager. Search for the user.
  4. Additional business attributes (such as purchasing org, cost centers) appear under Other User Info in Ariba.

Note: As mentioned, ODM schema attributes are mapped to Accounting attributes, and custom schema (SAP:Ariba) attributes are mapped to Shipping and Billing attributes.

 

Custom Ariba Cloud Identity Service Schema Migration Plan

As of this writing, the standard SAP Ariba schema (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:ariba:2.0:User) is not yet available. Once SAP releases it (currently planned for Q2 2025), follow these steps to migrate from your custom schema:

  1. Update Existing Users
    • Perform an Update operation on all Ariba users in Cloud Identity Service, replacing the custom schema (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:vendor:ariba:2.0:User) with the standard schema (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:ariba:2.0:User).
  2. Populate Attributes
    • Confirm that attributes are correctly mapped and populated in the new standard schema.
  3. Adjust Source and Target Transformation Files
    • Replace all references to the custom schema with the standard Ariba schema.
  4. Run IPS Jobs
    • Execute your IPS provisioning jobs again to ensure the updated schema references are correct and that data syncs as expected.

 

Conclusion

By following the steps outlined in this blog, you can successfully create and manage SAP Ariba users through SAP Cloud Identity Service, leveraging both the ODM schema and a custom Ariba schema (until the standard one is released). Identity Provisioning Service streamlines the process, allowing you to filter and transform user data from Identity Authentication into SAP Ariba. Once SAP releases the standard SAP Ariba schema, you can seamlessly migrate your custom Ariba schema, further simplifying your identity landscape and supporting broader initiatives like SAP Task Center and Joule.

entry.title: Re: Horizon Theme and Templates for Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @OzanCavusoglu ,

the Horizon theme you can already see in some demos is planned to be released for SAP Analytics Cloud with Q4 QRC 2025. At that point every end user will be able to choose their preferred theme in the user settings. The options will be the current theme and the four Horizon themes (morning, evening and high contrast white and black.

Best

Anna

entry.title: Re: Sap Analytics Cloud (SAC) - Show filter variable defi...

entry.title: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey - Part4 - Testing
entry.content:
Link back to index: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/blog-series-on-my-cpi-camel-learning-journe...

Ok, in this part we finally need to address the original subject/topic - manual and automated testing of our iflows (camel routes)

 

How we expect automated testing to work

First we need to go back to pro-code automated testing concepts.

Let's add our favourite figure yet another time here, but this time we will compare it with something pro-code - our tool itself.

For this purpose I wrote a really simple test for one of odata services our tool serves.

Typically I do it like that on my actual projects to have something like integration or end-to-end tests emulating the requests from ui.

This way I ensure that behaviour of backend matches expectations of frontend.

And this test is doing something really trivial:

  • Fetches list of packages (main page of Content tile/app)
  • Then navigates to package artifacts (Detail page)
  • And finally to Detail-Detail for integration_flow and script_collection artifacts to test if we have blueprint Content (that is displayed in code editor on frontend side)

You don't need to be a CAP or even JS developer, the main part here is to match the abstractions.

So, here's our test isolation diagram again:

So, for that odata service test, who is who?

Well, obviously, Code Under Test is our service (implementation of custom logic to be more  precise, cuz we don't typically test Generic CRUD cap provides out of the box). Meaning, it is literally some functions I have in srv/cpi-local.js 

Next, Test Case is obviously the test file we already mentioned (that simple-cpi-local.test.js guy).

And then we have some magic which is important to understand: we also have a Test Runner that provides us with some essential capabilities like mocking (jest in our case).

From previous posts we know that the tool basically is just a wrapper either for remote cpi services or for local file system.

In this case we (cpi-local) depend on Api service and Operations service. But we don't want to actually call them! We want to have full control of how our test behaves.

So we DO have Test Doubles - mocks, and those are the guys we have in jest.mock("..", () => { }) blocks.

What it does is it tricks node into loading not the original modules we have for our service implementations (from cds.requires section we have in package.json), but our mocks defined here.

And this "controls" line from Test Cases to Test Doubles is how we can literally control their state and/or responses.

Here I hardcoded all the stuff, but in real life my tests are more sophisticated (cuz I want to handle error cases etc), so my smart test doubles are indeed controlled from Test Cases (and sometimes I even extract them as separate reusable modules).

Ok, so why does it matter? Well, because our Code Under Test has no idea it deals with mocks rather than real services. And this is how we avoid modifying it.

Again, this is a very basic and trivial thing to say, but it is essential to automated testing.

For example, we can then consider writing another test for, let's say, ftp-local service.

But unfortunately, it is not testable unless we refactor it - it has dependencies to "fs" and instead of calling some functions provided by someone (like BundleHandler we also mock in cpi-local) it does direct calls to that.

And we already mentioned that: in order to have something tested, we start with designing it as testable!

But even if our cpi-local is testable, do we have a good test? NO!

Why? - well, because the service itself is a mere wrapper, so basically I don't test actual behaviour (because I mock all the stuff that is responsible for it, like aforementioned BundleHandler).

So in order to test something meaningful I need to ensure that BundleHandler guy himself is also testable and write proper Unit tests for it.

If we do npm run test:cpi-local we see coverage report for our tool, and it is only barely touching the service:

Again, this is a topic for another series, but at least we need to stress out the point that:

  • Not everything is testable
  • Even when something is testable, our tests still can suck (we write it to fail, not to stay green no matter what)
  • Test Runner is a magical glue connecting our pieces of puzzle: Test Case, Test Double and Code Under Test.

What about camel/osgi then?

Well, like I said earlier, Camel is proper Java development.

Therefore we would expect to find those abstractions we mentioned in previous sections there as well.

And indeed, there are plenty of ways to do real testing in Camel. But with Java of course (JUnit or something they have there...).

For example, this link tells us about blueprint testing: https://dzone.com/articles/some-tips-for-camel-blueprint-junit-testing

But again, in order to keep CUT unmodified, we need mocks (to replace all those adapters I currently comment out in local blueprints and satisfy other deps we entirely comment out at the top of blueprint)!

And yass we can has them:

https://camel.apache.org/components/3.22.x/mock-component.html

https://sling.apache.org/documentation/development/osgi-mock.html 
https://github.com/ops4j/org.ops4j.pax.exam
 
And in fact, all that stuff is so java, that I am seriously thinking that rewriting capic with CAP-Java is a right way to go to make it a proper product.
For example, in karaf docs they say that Karaf can also be run "embedded" (It also supports the "run anywhere" concept ... using the embedded mode), so imagine it running under cap-java (like we currently have ftp server running under our cap-nodejs).
 
So I currently feel like I am doing some kind of proof of concept to pitch it later to someone (cuz obviously, it is an effort of another level, to implement adapters and other stuff for mocks etc)
 

Ok, but do we have at least anything without going full-Java mode?

Well, kinda.
And because we don't have a test runner, we have our test cases and code under test blended together.
Cuz the only way for us to mock something - is to replace it with other stuff )
But maybe we can do better?
Well, maybe. For example, after I discovered that jms iflow uses some interceptFrom magic, I decided to take a closer look at it.
And what I discovered was that indeed I could keep some parts of original bliueprint like this one:
<camel:to id="MessageFlow_13_347279183257109" uri="sap-processdirect:foo?address=/custom/GenericReceiverDataStore"/>
 
But to direct it to our Generic Receiver I would add this clause to our CamelContext
<camel:interceptSendToEndpoint uri="sap-processdirect:foo?address=/custom/GenericReceiverDataStore" skipSendToOriginalEndpoint="true">
<camel:to uri="direct-vm:/Common_Generic_Receiver"/>
</camel:interceptSendToEndpoint>
 
Notice the skipSendToOriginalEndpoint parameter.
Great, but does it make it better?
To be honest, not so much, as we still need to resolve dependencies to original sap-processdirect component and we still must modify the blueprint itself.
 
And thus we can conclude that unless we can run our original blueprint in a controlled environment with injected  test doubles replacing some of original dependencies, we cannot close that gap to our desired automated testing architecture (those three guys on our favourite diagram + invisible fourth one we uncovered today, Test Runner).
Because again, once we start directly modifying our blueprint, we ruin all the abstractions.
 

What are our options?

Well, remember we were discussing equivalence?

Meaning, if our iflow does some kind of transformation of message, and we can refer to it as some kind of function (ideally pure, but in most cases of course with side effects like changed state in external systems we integrate with), then we can do the following:

  • manually create "snapshots" of it as local blueprint,
  • test/debug it using local tooling
  • produce inputs/outputs that test the expected behaviour of iflow
  • preserve those inputs/outputs somewhere
  • later at any point replay all those messages in actual iflow.

That would look pretty much like unit testing, right? Or maybe even like regression testing

But again, it would only make sense if and only if remote and local blueprints ARE equivalent in terms of expected behaviour.

And as promised, lets see how that new testing diagram could look like and who would become who.

Like I mentioned in previous part, I am not sure for now if local blueprint is a 1-to-1 relation to our iflow, or as with proper automated tests we can have 1-to-many, but for now I guess this kinda makes sense to me.

But to be completely honest, I am not even 100% this is a proper abstraction - it is just a way I currently feel about it, while I keep exploring and learning stuff )

Another important notice here would be - even though I call capic a "Test Runner" here, it is definitely NOT the same abstraction as for example jest would be to cap, because it does not control environment in any way.

Therefore it just deals with either ftp (and therefore karaf) to run stuff locally, OR calls cpi iflow endpoints to test remote processing.

Very, very simple stuff here (and still not so clear abstractions imo).

But Isolation?

Yes, that's a valid point.

In case of a local blueprint we do manually mock our external systems to isolate ourselves, therefore we indeed can say that we are isolated.

But when we replay messages on CPI, we still DO have real external systems interacting with our Code Under Test.

Unfortunately, currently I don't see feasible options to deal with that, other than by design of an iflow itself.

But I believe in some cases it will just leave us splitting it all into zillions of one- or two- step chunks (calling each other via process direct) in order to do that kind of "unit-testing" which most likely will not make any sense from the practical perspective (cuz we will still need some wrappers on cpi side to have endpoints exposed to our tool).

Maybe once I have more real-life experience in terms of "overall feeling" of what actually happens there most of the time (after all, it is a different development paradigm), I will have some ideas how to improve my abstractions - or will figure out that there was a good reason why no such tools like mine existed 😉

For now let's maybe have a final demo in this chapter to see how it could work based on my current understanding of all this.

Demo part

We will try linking some in-out messages produced locally for iflows from our samples where we established equivalence, then re-run it both locally and remotely.

Spoiler
iflowreplayablecomment
Basics_Exception_SubprocessFTP-, CPI-Have "atom:updated" Feed element from ahc adapter
Basics_Sender_Initiated_ExternalFTP+, CPI+But useless as it is just an echo
Basics_Attachments_Create-No equivalence
Basics_XML_To_WSDL_MappingFTP-, CPI-Have currentDate side effect in mapping
Basics_XSLT_MappingFTP+, CPI+Pure transormation (as long as webshop data stays the same)
Basics_XPATH_And_ConditionsFTP+, CPI-*Groovy script produced xml with different order of props, will have delta with CPI (can be fixed though)
Basics_XML_To_JSON_MappingFTP-, CPI-Could be literally pure function, but currentDate have side effect in mapping as well

As table shows, every iflow except for xslt one will not be replayable on cpi after we record and link data locally.
What the tool can maybe do instead of just testing strict equality is calculate some "smart diff" as result so that human decides if it passes or not (maybe as separate semi-automated mode).

00:00 Intro and testing related abstractions again (boring)

03:55 Quick setup recap (all stuff rezipped from samples)

05:10 Testing/demo routine explained on Basics_Exception_Subprocess iflow

08:17 Basics_Sender_Initiated_External iflow

10:11 Basics_Attachments_Create iflow

12:04 3 out of 3 and Basics_XML_To_WSDL_Mapping iflow

15:11 Some hope and Basics_XSLT_Mapping iflow

17:13 Basics_XPATH_And_Conditions iflow

19:45 Basics_XML_To_JSON_Mapping iflow

22:14 // and the problem was we introduced all those side effects (currentDate stuff) ourselves in our mapping killing the testability

23:48 Results table and conclusion

entry.title: Re: Sap Analytics Cloud (SAC) - Show filter variable defi...
entry.content: Hi @deshmukh I tried to create an fx node in the fact but the variable is not accessible:

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content: what to do if certificates not listing with userID

entry.title: Re: ABAP AUTHORITY CHECK Explained: A Practical Guide with Real-World Example
entry.content:
Okay... This is an extensive blog post trying to cover a lot of ground related to the authorization concept. And because of that the text kind of becomes its own enemy and result is a bit difficult to follow. Below are some improvement suggestions.

  • Please, please use Pretty Printer to format the code. The misaligned code is very difficult to read.
  • The numbering is all over the place. P. 1 is followed by... another 1, etc. Such text volume really needs to be split in sections/chapters. Modularization is not just for ABAP. 🙂
  • "field" is mistyped as "filed" (different word) in many places. ChatGPT can fix this easily.
  • There are well-known transactions that can be used to troubleshoot any authorization issues, so no need to bother "BASIS people". SU53 shows all failed auth checks and ST01 has an awesome option for authorization trace.
  • The code seems off to me but I'm not sure what you were going for. For example, after authorization check, it has IF sy-subrc <> 0, then error message and then... more code. Did you miss ELSE somewhere? What's the point of having code after the error message? It would never work. Very confused on this one (and this is in more than one example).
  • I suspect quite a few readers would be confused by what is "cylinder" (I have no idea myself). There isn't a real need for such a long example IMHO. All the additional code doesn't make the point any clearer. If anyone wants to see very simple example used in our ABAP Introduction book, they are here on Github.
  • Code example for "delivery charges" is also very oddly structured. Why are you doing SELECT and LOOP and all that on selection screen? It's not how the real life code would look like.
  • It would probably be helpful to add some links to SAP Help and mention why SAP documentation on this is not clear / not sufficient (I'm just guessing if it was clear this post wouldn't be needed).

Also, I didn't see it mention in the text but to clarify, we don't HAVE to create custom authorization objects all the time. It's OK to use existing objects if they make sense. I find many ABAPers are confused by that and think that every Z table must have its own object. Also, a typical source of confusion for functional folks: assigning object to role does nothing if there is no ABAP code that does the authority check.

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: “With ‘Joule for Developers’ We Have a Treasure in Our Hands”
entry.content:
"if you’re not working with the tool yet, give it a try! " - sure, we would love to but how exactly do you suggest anyone does that?

This is not include in ABAP developer trial and "general" availability is so limited that hardly anyone could possibly have access to it. Is this available to S/4HANA on-premise or private cloud customers without an expensive SAP BTP service subscription?

This "treasure" might as well be hidden behind bullet-proof glass in a museum.

entry.title: Re: Community project: Selection of classic UI tools
entry.content: Thanks for sharing, @jrgkraus ! I'd kill for this tool 10+ years ago but hey, it's better late than never. 🙂

entry.title: Using CDS and Fiori Elements to see derived roles
entry.content:
Trying to follow which transaction codes are used by a specific PFCG role is quite easy to find with SUIM, but navigating from derived roles to imparting roles is not as straight forward.

I decided to generate some CDS views and a Fiori Elements app to rectify this.

The source can be imported from https://github.com/michaelnicholls/ZTCD_ROLES.git

Once the correct service bindings are activated and published, the BSP app can then be used to find roles via transaction code to show any imparting and/or derived roles that have a specified set of transaction codes.

Please see the following example.

I have assumed that only single TCD values are used in the authorization values.

entry.title: Restricted Interchangeability of Parts in MRP
entry.content:
I have a requirement from users where we have parts listed in our BOM that have alternate materials that can be used.  Since moving to S4 we now have access to the automotive functionality of Interchangeability of parts.  I have configured all this for full interchangeability for some components and MRP is showing like i want with all interchangeable materials displayed together for stock and movements. 

But I recently learned that we have some particular components that are not fully interchangeable.  They are one way interchangeable.  I can use them interchangeably for some of our FERT's, but not for others.  I have created a number of FFF classes and set these up as well as set up the one way restricted interchangeability.

I have also configured the customising to allow Restricted Interchangeability (Logistics - General → Interchangeability of Parts → Define Settings for Material Exchange and Restricted Interchangeability)

When i go into a production order with a restricted interchangeable component and use the "Trigger material exchange" the list of available materials works perfectly with only showing the interchangeable materials set up as fully interchangeable or restricted.  Perfect.

However, when i run MD04, this still only considers the fully interchangeable materials.  The restricted interchangeable materials that i see available in the production order are not displayed.  Looking at the customising to allow Restricted Interchangeability, i can't see any option for it to be included in MRP.  

I am SOO close to having a perfect solution, if i can just get the restricted interchangeability working for MRP/MD04.

There doesn't seem to be a lot of documentation around for this, so i am unsure if i have reached the limitation of this functionality and its not expected to work in MRP or not.  Has anyone used this functionality and have more of an understanding of its limitations and whether what i'm expecting is achievable?

entry.title: Re: Invoke Generative AI Hub Models using @sap-ai-sdk/ai-...
entry.content:
I would need to check how it is done in JavaScript SDK, because in Python SDK it is enough to set the environment variables or a config file `~/.aicore/config.json` as per https://help.sap.com/doc/generative-ai-hub-sdk/CLOUD/en-US/_reference/README_sphynx.html#configuration.

Then the code automatically initiates a client with credentials from those configurations, without an explicit read from env variables or from that config file in your code.

But that's how it is done in Python, and I would need to check how it is done in JS.

entry.title: Re: Changing the Default (Predelivered) Email template fo...
entry.content: Thanks so much Dequan - I was missing the Output Determination Parameters step (hadn't scrolled far enough to the right to review the assigned template #facepalm)

entry.title: Settlement in mass for orders in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
We would like to know if it is possible to settle multiple orders at the same time in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Settlement in mass for orders in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


In order to run settlement for multiple orders on the same time, you can make use of job template "Actual Settlement: Orders" in Schedule Overhead Accounting Jobs app. Kindly check the documentation below for additional details:

Schedule Overhead Accounting Jobs
Schedule Actual Settlement


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: CdsArrayedTypeImpl cannot be cast to class com.sap.cds.reflect.CdsSimpleType
entry.content: I get the some error. Any leads?

entry.title: HTTP response '502: Bad Gateway. Unable to open connection to backend system'
entry.content:
Get the below error while connecting from SAP CPI iflow to backend ERP via cloud connector.

HTTP response '502: Bad Gateway. Unable to open connection to backend system' http://wa2xxxx.xxx.com:8443/sap/bc/srt/idoc?sap-client=300

URL is 'reachable' in BTP cockpit.

It works in QA but does not work in PRD.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Proxying as an associate
entry.content:
I have had success using a direct link to LMS instead of clicking a Quick Action card/using the menu. 

Set up your poxy, and from the home page, copy/paste the direct link to the LMS in the browser's address bar. 

You can use this to figure out how to build the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/5c6733335c434d6f81210378bbea6405/478def93ab4f4ad4a3ecf6ad730bc6c5.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Table Logs
entry.content: Please add more information on the question. It's not clear what you want to achieve.

entry.title: Re: Redefine or Extend FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER
entry.content: I don't get it where the first image was taken from.

entry.title: Item Master Data - Unit Price Field Missing
entry.content:
SAP Business One 10 But we are unable to insert and price amount in Item Master Data. Price Unit Field and Price List Field are not displayed for us to insert our price. Can anyone help find the solution to this problem?

We use US Simple COA (chart of account). Please see the screen for the missing fields:

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace -Send shipment status to customer/buyer using easy r
entry.content:
hi @md_ab , 

If the email id is a custom field, then it must be sent from backend system(S/4HANA). GTT relay on backend data to be passed. You can easily extend GTT extractor to add custom fields- look at my previous blog on how to do it.

entry.title: Re: How to set the BOM to Change Master Required when cre...
entry.content: Hello, thank you so much for your response. In the part you answered, I check that the master change required option is enabled for the relevant BOM type in the BOM Settings Management (SCM_BOM) path. May I know the detailed path for setting? Also, please check the page of the link 1 and 2 below because it is not possible to check the page. Thank you. Have a good day.

entry.title: Change currency in "Maintenance Order Costs" reports
entry.content:
We need to check the costs from app "Maintenance Order Costs" in MXN, that is our Company Code currency, but currently it appears in USD (Global currency). How to view the costs in Company Code currency?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change currency in "Maintenance Order Costs" reports
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


By default, the app "Maintenance Order Costs" shows the Planned/Actuals Costs in Global currency. Although, you can go to 'Settings' button and select the fields "Planned Costs in CC" and "Actual Costs in CC", in this way, the report will shown the values in your Company Code currency.

Maintenance Order Costs


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: did you resolve the issue, As we face the same issue
Error message: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persist
entry.content: I could not subscribe BPA. Please assist

entry.title: Folder level data restriction/filtering
entry.content: I have BI43 SP4 on Linux.  I wanted to know if I have created a folders ABC and XYZ and user A100 has access to both folders. If User A100 logs in to BOXI and run any report in Folder ABC then it should add extra join or condition (ABC is NULL) in report logic/sql in that folder. Similar for folder XYZ where it should add extra join or condition (XYZis NULL) in report logic/sql in that folder. Is there a way to achieve this where same user will have data restrictions depending on folder it is accessing.

entry.title: Re: A 25x faster mta.yaml build for SAP CAP Node.js projects
entry.content:
@cguttikonda24, sure it does! This is even better when deploying from CI/CD pipelines as you don't need to wait for 10 minutes to see the results.

It doesn't matter $ npm root returns, the result isn't used for anything. It's just a placeholder, you could execute anything or nothing at all 😊

entry.title: Re: SAC - Blending Support in Optimized Design Mode
entry.content: I still do not have linked analysis in Optimized mode. When is this expected to be released or in which version can I get it I am on 2025.1.9

entry.title: Re: A 25x faster mta.yaml build for SAP CAP Node.js projects
entry.content:
@Harjyot, in terms of speed and efficiency, npm-ci will always be worse than what shared here. Regardless whether it's local or in a CI/CD environment. The technique presented uses the result of $ cds build --production skipping the extra steps like npm-ci whenever possible.

I've spent time exploring ways to improve build/deploy times mostly because I got sick of waiting forever for my CI/CD pipelines to complete. It got much worse when I was creating a new GitHub Action to deploy to SAP BTP. I had to build/deploy multiple times to test everything. Waiting 10+ minutes for every change I made wasn't sustainable.

entry.title: Re: A 25x faster mta.yaml build for SAP CAP Node.js projects
entry.content: @marcel_schork, that's awesome! I tried with empty/null values and didn't work, MBT would fail. Good to know we can pass an empty array. Thanks for sharing 😊

entry.title: Fiori tile in BTP for a custom TCode in on-premise system
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a custom transaction in my on premise system, and I would like to know if I could create a Fiori tile for this tcode on my BTP environment. If that is possible appreciate any guidance on the same.

Thank you

entry.title: DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB SAPLCKMS CKMLCP
entry.content:
执行CKMLCP过账步骤过程中DUMP，请问这要怎么解决。

ST22查看信息如下

类别 ABAP 编程错误

运行时错误 DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR

异常 CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB

ABAP 程序 SAPLCKMS

应用组件 CO-PC-ACT

短文本
Open SQL array insert produces duplicate records in the database.

发生了什么？
Error in the ABAP application program.

The current ABAP program "SAPLCKMS" had to be terminated because it found a
statement that could not be executed.

我能做什么？
Note which actions and entries caused the error to occur.

Consult your SAP administrator.

Using transaction ST22 for ABAP dump analysis, you can view, manage,
and retain termination messages for longer periods.

 

错误分析
During COMMIT WORK processing, an exception has occurred that could not
be intercepted during processing. To avoid inconsistencies, this
situation causes the session to abort, regardless of whether or not
there is an exception handler outside the COMMIT WORK statement. The
reason for the exception occurring was:
When an Open SQL array insert is performed, trying to insert a record
into the database table "CKMLPP" causes the function to terminate if a
record with the same key already exists.

(When an Open SQL individual record insert is performed, this situation
does not cause the function to terminate. SY-SUBRC is set to 4 instead.)

如何更正此错误
COMMIT WORK processing cannot be interrupted. If the exception cannot
be prevented, "CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB" must be caught when processing the COMM
WORK. To
prevent the exception, note the following:

Open SQL array insert should only be used if it is certain that none of
the records specified already exists on the database. If this cannot be
guaranteed, exception CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB should be intercepted, and the
error must be resolved.
If the error occurs in a non-modfied SAP program, you might be able to
find a solution in the SAP Notes system. If you have access to the SAP
Notes system, check there first using the following keywords:

"DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR" CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB
"SAPLCKMS" bzw. LCKMSU33
"CKMS_BUFFER_POST_DYN"
If you cannot solve the problem yourself, please send the following
information to SAP:

1. The description of the problem (short dump)
To do this, choose System -> List -> Save -> Local File (unconverted)
on the screen you are in now.

2. The relevant system log
To do this, call the system log in transaction SM21. Restrict the time
interval to ten minutes before the short dump and five minutes after
it. In the display, choose System -> List -> Save -> Local File
(unconverted).

3. If these are programs of your own, or modified SAP programs: The
source text of the programs
To do this, please choose
"Other Utilties -> Upload/Download -> Download".

4. Details regarding the conditions under which the error occurred or
which actions and input caused the error.

源代码提取

行 代码

1 FUNCTION ckms_buffer_post_dyn.
2 *"--------------------------------------------------------
3 *"*"Verbuchungsfunktionsbaustein:
4 *"
5 *"*"Lokale Schnittstelle:
6 *" IMPORTING
7 *" VALUE(T_DYN_CKMLPP) TYPE CKMLPP_SORTED
8 *" VALUE(T_DYN_CKMLCR) TYPE CKMLCR_SORTED
9 *"--------------------------------------------------------
10 *4.6C
11 *CS ALRK238430 230999 neu angelegt
12
>>>>> INSERT ckmlpp FROM TABLE t_dyn_ckmlpp.
14 IF sy-subrc <> 0.
15 MESSAGE x099 WITH 'CKMS_BUFFER_POST_DYN' '1' space.
16 ENDIF.
17 INSERT ckmlcr FROM TABLE t_dyn_ckmlcr.
18 IF sy-subrc <> 0.
19 MESSAGE x099 WITH 'CKMS_BUFFER_POST_DYN' '2' space.

20 ENDIF.
21 REFRESH: t_dyn_ckmlpp, t_dyn_ckmlcr.
22
23 ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Re: Modern JavaScript testing frameworks with Fiori/UI5 – Vitest
entry.content:
@junweitoh, sorry for the ultra-delayed response, but I missed this notification among all the noise...

You don't have to load from the CDN, but you must load the UI5 files from somewhere. As said in the blog post: "We’re going to use the openui5-sample-app which already has QUnit tests, therefore we can convert them to Vitest and compare the results."

I'm not creating any new tests, just converting the old QUnit ones for the sake of comparison. In this case, there's no DOM interaction, but you could have. Trying document.findElementById('button').click() would work just fine and that's exactly the whole point of those browser emulators.

entry.title: Re: Maintain container with or without container unit
entry.content: Hi, would you be able to provide a screenshot of the Container Management functionality you mentioned as I could not find the app from both the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library and our SAP S/4HANA system?

entry.title: URL link updating when enable SAP IAS for SuccessFactor
entry.content: hello experts, hope you have a good day, I have issue about IAS and SF link, previously client has lots of SF URL links in Enterprise Portal and users can directly click on the links to jump to the corresponding page content of SF(not via deep link), however currently client enable IAS for SuccessFactors, and user can't click on the links to jump to right page due to IAS authentication rather than jump to SF company ID confirm page, client don't want to update URL links in Portal because they have lots of links, it is big task for them, do we have any ideas to make it no longer necessary to confirm the company ID page for authentication login without changing the URL content? or some technial methods?

entry.title: Re: Error while sending the e-mail message. The attachment could not be accessed
entry.content: I wanted to comment that I have been trying to set this up for a couple days now and point 4 above is what solved it for me. I had set the mounts up via IP but not hostname. Adding an /etc/hosts entry and setting the mounts up via hostname (because the SAP attachment share was //ServerName/Attachments) was the fix. Thank You!

entry.title: Re: India Localization Compliance Reports in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, 

May I know for GST tax calculation which scope item is required apart from 1J2 which can be utilized for reporting?

entry.title: Re: Sap Analytics Cloud (SAC) - Show filter variable defi...
entry.content: sorry for not mentioning clearly. You need to create Transaction Currency input parameter in fact view and not in Analytical model and then finally create calculated column like I mentioned earlier.

entry.title: Professional Services：Cost Recognition for Periodic Service (SPPC) question
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My client's service contract uses recognition code SPPC. The client has a question: assuming the contract period is from January 2025 to December 2025, if there is an unplanned temporary expense in May, they want to recognize the entire expense in May's cost, reflecting an unexpected loss in the contract for that month, rather than spreading it over the contract period. Is there a feasible way to do this?   

 

Best regards
Lance

entry.title: Re: Open Excel File from PAPM Cloud in Analysis for Office
entry.content:
Hi Daniela, There is no native functionality available in PaPM cloud. However you can follow this blog to get some reference for your requirement.

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-papm-cloud-integration-with-microsoft-excel/ba-p/13568106

Regards,

Akshay

entry.title: Re: Limit purchase order (new purchase order type)
entry.content:
I

would like to clarify which account suitable for me;1) Enterprise account2) Standard account. i have total 180 document PO for invoice processing per month. Any transaction limit for Standard account ?

entry.title: How to deploy extended standard legacy Fiori apps in BAS ?
entry.content:
Hello I am trying to extend the standard Fiori app F2023 using fiori adaptation project generator in BAS wich says i need to make an extention project instead.

So i do that i extend the intended views and controllers and it all works localy, but when i want to deploy it the wizard asks me to make a new fiori app and a new tile instead of extending the standard app.

And even then when i go for it the deployed application is not working as i get a blank screen and error in console: 

cannot read property of "content" in index.html

or 

Error in app component zeam_malf_mans1: No descriptor was found

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: C_TS4FI_2023 and C_S4CFI_2408
entry.content:
I recently passed the C_TS4FI_2023 certification, and I found it to be a great way to validate my knowledge of SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting. My main reason for pursuing this certification was to enhance my expertise in financial processes and improve my career prospects in SAP finance roles.

When comparing C_TS4FI_2023 and C_S4CFI_2408, the key difference lies in the implementation focus. C_TS4FI_2023 covers core financial accounting in an on-premise environment, while C_S4CFI_2408 is tailored for cloud-based implementations. If you're looking to specialize in traditional SAP finance, C_TS4FI_2023 might be a better fit, whereas C_S4CFI_2408 is ideal for professionals working with SAP Cloud solutions.

To prepare, I used P2PCerts for practice questions and study materials. Their resources were extremely helpful, providing real exam-like questions that made it easier to understand complex topics. Passing this certification has significantly boosted my job opportunities and made me more competitive in the SAP job market.
My resource was: https://www.p2pcerts.com/

entry.title: Re: Update PMR item Status with in EWM during consumption...
entry.content: Hi naresh_kudipudi13 - Did you try to stop via interface debugging (Proces- QueueDeregister... Stopping...etc) ...I am believe this is function module would give you desire things which you are looking. Before conclude debug once. Thanks! Kind Regards, Ajit

entry.title: Is linked analysis possible in Optimized mode SAC Live model ?
entry.content: I am getting the option greyed out when trying to do a linked analysis. We have a S4HANA live data model. Any pointers will be helpful

entry.title: Re: Key areas of change in C_TS4FI_2023 SAP S/4HANA Financials
entry.content: The C_TS4FI_2023 SAP S/4HANA Financials certification has introduced several key updates, aligning with the latest advancements in SAP financial processes. The exam now places greater emphasis on Universal Journal Enhancements, Financial Closing Automation, and Embedded Analytics for Real-Time Reporting, reflecting SAP’s shift toward intelligent finance. Additionally, there’s an increased focus on Accounts Payable and Receivable Optimization, ensuring candidates understand streamlined processes in SAP S/4HANA. To effectively prepare for these changes, P2PCerts.com provides up-to-date study materials and practice exams, helping candidates master the latest topics with real exam-like questions. These updates make it crucial to stay informed and practice with reliable resources to ensure success in the certification exam.

entry.title: Re: Update PMR item Status with in EWM during consumption posting
entry.content:
Hi Ajit, 

You are right. I stopped the queue and debugged to see that this fm creates and updates PMR document based on the changes made on Processing order either create or change. However, it appears to me that parameters at item level doesn’t contain the status field and its value or deletion indicator field XLOEK to update. Most of the blogs points to say that this kind of updates needs to be flown from s4 end from PO but shouldn’t be directly be done in EWM. As I mentioned I have the alternative to update custom fields and use them for staging. We are trying to avoid that by changing the standard fields so that the standard behavior of staging will drop those PMR lines for further selection. Hope to see if any one had similar experience..

Thanks for your support so far..

Naresh k

entry.title: Is MB5B used in S4 Hana?
entry.content:
Hello,

  Can you tell me if Tcode MB5B was discontinued in S4 Hana.  My company did an upgrade last year and now the data is not updating since the upgrade?  I checked the Tcode comparisions from SAP to S4 Hana and I didn't find MB5B listed as a different Tcode in S4 Hana.  The IT group is telling me it is no longer used in S4 Hana.  Any help is appreciated.

Thank you

Sean

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Plan Delivery Time for Purchase requisition i...
entry.content:
The Planned delivery time is a fixed value in MRP2 View of material master data or in PIR, so if you want to Variant configuration characteristics value to calculate, you should customize a program and logic to change this filed before running MRP.

Material master data:

PIR:

entry.title: Re: How to set the BOM to Change Master Required when cre...
entry.content:
To make the Change Master mandatory for BOM creation and modification:

1. Path to Configuration:
Navigate to the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
Open the Manage BOM Settings application.
In the SCM_BOM section, locate the setting for "Change Master Required."
Enable this setting for the relevant BOM types you are working with.

2. Verify Configuration:
Ensure that the Change Master settings are correctly configured and activated for the necessary BOM types. Check that the required change numbers are assigned to the BOM and that you’re using the correct change number during creation or modification.

reference docu:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/be1f26557c9d40338a721b95829569f6/8307c453f57eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?version=6.03.latest

entry.title: Re: Cascading Budget and Planner Hierarchy
entry.content: @pbrandi Yes it also works with rollup, had it confirmed by our Product Manager today.

entry.title: Re: Budget hold back in Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi @vinkamath 

As far as I know there is no holdback option with any kind of budget in Variable Pay. You would need to look at maybe creating a 2nd budget box if that even works.

Budgets in Variable Pay work in a very specific way and custom field logic will not give you the same exact results than in Compensation.

Please see this recent other thread here: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/variable-pay-budget-calculation/qaq-p/14019820

and links that I shared with Olga including one to a page with very detailled explanation of every single type of budget possible.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: how to stop migration cockpit long time running job
entry.content: during the project implementation, the data deletion action is sensitive, here I would propose to raise the oss ticket to SAP company for further deletion action.

entry.title: Re: Prototype Design Pattern in SAP OOABAP
entry.content:
Hi @Jelena_Perfiljeva 
Thank you for your honest feedback! I understand that the example might not have clearly conveyed the real-world applicability of the Prototype Pattern in SAP.

The key purpose of this pattern is to efficiently create object copies when instantiating them from scratch is costly. A practical SAP example would be duplicating business objects in SAP RAP or BOPF. For instance, in a procurement scenario, if a purchase order needs to be replicated with slight modifications (e.g., different vendor terms or adjusted quantities), cloning an existing object allows us to retain most attributes while modifying only specific fields. This avoids redundant object creation and unnecessary database reads.

Regarding the coding style, I agree that cl_demo_output=>display is a better approach for output, and I’ll review Clean ABAP guidelines to refine the implementation. Also, you’re right—just "ABAP" is sufficient. I appreciate the attention to detail!

I’ll consider updating the blog with a more SAP-relevant example to make the pattern’s benefits clearer. Thanks again for your valuable input!

entry.title: Re: Local Client copy error - CL_MDG_TR_CC, CL_FINB_TR_CC
entry.content: how did you resolve this issue? im getting the same error on my new system.

entry.title: Re: Hyper-V virtualization is not supported for SAP syste...
entry.content:
Hi Sitlesh,

1. Microsoft Hyper-V supports Microsoft OS. Linux is not supported with Hyper-V on-premises but is supported with the Azure Cloud environment.

2. You can migrate all your SAP systems to Microsoft Windows servers using MS SQL or any database or go for Cloud solutions.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: how do we automate space & org members access in SAP...
entry.content: Hi Tong, in CLI i could see an option to add one user to one space. Is there an option/cmd to add a user to all the available space at a time?

entry.title: Re: Mass Update of Max Stock Level from Excel File Using MM46 in SAP S/4HANA Retail
entry.content:
Hello,

​In SAP Fiori, the equivalent functionality to the classic SAP GUI transaction MM46 (Mass Maintenance for Article Master Data) is provided by the "Mass Maintenance" app. This app enables users to update multiple master data records simultaneously and can be accessed via the "Start Mass Maintenance" and "Manage Mass Maintenance" tiles in the Fiori launchpad.

SAP Reference:
https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2505')/S15OP

entry.content:

I have got one requirement to fetch the Compensation design worksheet fields via OData API entity. Is there any entity to fetch the Compensation design worksheet fields.

 

Best Regards,

Pankaj

 

#SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central #SAP SuccessFactors Compensation #SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Re: End - to - End Guide for using Multi-Select Position Generic Object on Job Requisition
entry.content:
Hi @NadiaZapata,

As this solution work for any no of hiring but in case of 1300 position it will be hard to recruitment team to select the all those particular position and taking offer approval one by one which will become a long process 
So if for this much of hiring better to go with Evergreen Requisition kind of Hiring 

Regards,
Arun Singh

entry.title: Re: SQL Trace (ST05): Enhancements to the SAP HANA Plan Visualizer
entry.content:
Thank you Adrian for sharing this detailed blog!

I have a question to the general usage of ST05, do you know if ST05 has the ability to trace SQL query on custom Z_view in $TMP package?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: End - to - End Guide for using Multi-Select Position Generic Object on Job Requisition
entry.content:
Hi @steffiedsa2 

Usually this solution is for the same kind of position, having the same attributes of position 
As if you looking for the different location then in this case we also have to make location object Multiselect where we can select multiple location and also with that we have make offer detail templated as per the countries pay structure As if different country them Pay grade and paycomponent could be different

So this kind of case is possible to achieve but it need a proper configuration with keeping all the scenarios and recruitment process  

Regards,
Arun Singh

entry.title: Re: TPM15M for long-term contracts
entry.content: Hi, which note that you found saying that :" the transfer between accounting accounts occurs only for movements that impact the position"? I am facing the same issue where the TPM15M transfer nominal amount from LT to ST valuation class but didn't generate any accounting. Some posts suggested to use TPM1, creating a new position procedure for current portion transfer instead of using TPM15M. Please share your findings if you are able to resolve your issue. Thank you.

entry.title: onInit hook is not executing from controller
entry.content:
I have created the below js Controller with the name PDMDocumentListActions.controller.js in ext/controller folder in app folder.
Fiori element generated through the Fiori application generator in vs code

 

sap.ui.define(
  ['sap/m/MessageToast', 'sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel', 'sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller'],
  function (MessageToast, JSONModel, Controller) {
    'use strict';

    return Controller.extend('router.ext.controller.PDMDocumentListActions', {
      onDeactivateDocument: function (oEvent) {
        var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
        var sPath = oEvent.getSource().getBindingContext().getPath();

        // Call CAP backend action
        oModel.callFunction('/deactivateDocument', {
          method: 'POST',
          urlParameters: {
            docId: oModel.getProperty(sPath + '/docId')
          },
          success: function () {
            MessageToast.show('Document deactivated successfully.');
            oModel.refresh();
          },
          error: function (oError) {
            MessageToast.show('Error deactivating document.');
          }
        });
      },

      onGetDocumentByDocId: function () {
        MessageToast.show('Custom handler invoked.');
      },

      onInit: function () {
        console.log('PDMDcoumentListActions controller initialized.');
        var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();
        var that = this;

        //Fetch user roles from backend service
        oModel.callFunction('/getUserRoles', {
          method: 'GET',
          success: function (oData) {
            console.log('User roles fetched successfully', oData + ' ' + oData.value);
            that._setButtonVisibility(oData.value);
          },
          error: function (oError) {
            console.error('Error fetching user roles', oError);
          }
        });
      },

      _setButtonVisibility: function (aRoles) {
        var isEntityManager = aRoles.includes('EntityManager');

        var oUIModel = new JSONModel({
          showActions: isEntityManager
        });

        this.getView().setModel(oUIModel, 'ui');
      }
    });
  }
);

 

In manifest.json , I have added the below content in"sap.ui5"

 

 "extends": {
      "extensions": {
        "sap.ui.controllerExtensions": {
          "sap.fe.templates.ListReport.ListReportController": {
            "controllerName": "router.ext.controller.PDMDocumentListActions"
          }
        }
      }
    }

 

 These are the below action added in manifest.json

 

 "content": {
                "header": {
                  "actions": {
                    "getDocumentByDocId": {
                      "id": "getDocumentByDocId",
                      "press": "router.ext.controller.PDMDocumentListActions.onGetDocumentByDocId",
                      "visible": "{ui>/showActions}",
                      "enabled": true,
                      "text": "Get Document"
                    },
                    "deactivateDocument": {
                      "id": "deactivateDocument",
                      "press": "router.ext.controller.PDMDocumentListActions.onDeactivateDocument",
                      "visible": "{ui>/showActions}",
                      "enabled": true,
                      "text": "Deactivate Document"
                    }
                  }
                }
              }

 

 The below is the controller handler in cap Java,

 

package customer.poc_ams_policy.handlers;

import java.util.List;
import java.util.stream.Collectors;

import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
import org.springframework.stereotype.Component;

import com.sap.cds.services.handler.EventHandler;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.annotations.On;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.annotations.ServiceName;
import com.sap.cds.services.request.UserInfo;

import cds.gen.documentservice.GetUserRolesContext;

@Component
@ServiceName("DocumentService")
public class UserRoleHandler implements EventHandler {

    private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(UserRoleHandler.class);

    @On(event = "getUserRoles")
    public void getUserRoles(GetUserRolesContext context) {
        UserInfo userInfo = context.getUserInfo();
        List<String> roles = userInfo.getRoles().stream().collect(Collectors.toList());
        logger.info("User roles: {}", roles);
        context.setResult(roles);
    }

}

 

Please let me know where I went wrong in above code even first line 

console.log('PDMDcoumentListActions controller initialized.'); of onInit also not printing.

error:
Log-dbg.js:497  [FUTURE FATAL] sap.fe.templates.ListReport.ListReportController: The registered Event Listener 'onInit' must not have a return value. -

entry.title: Entry is not a valid supply chain unit (/SCWM/SHP_RCV)
entry.content: Hi there! I need help with error /SCWM/SHP_RCV in srt_moni. My freight order does not get in ewm after I do posting in TM.

entry.title: Re: How to deploy extended standard legacy Fiori apps in...
entry.content:
Hi Ayoub,

The standard app F2023 is a Transactional application and not built on SAP Fiori Elements. Therefore, it's expected that the wizard suggests creating an Extension Project instead of an Adaptation Project for modifications.

Yes, we should deploy it to the ABAP repository using a different name, typically starting with Z or Y to distinguish it as a custom object. The namespace is automatically assigned as: customer.<project_name>

After deployment, create a custom tile and ensure that the target mapping ID matches the ID specified in the Component.js file of the custom app in BAS.

*zreportrepair-custom name given to project.

entry.title: Re: Fiori tile in BTP for a custom TCode in on-premise sy...
entry.content:
Please refer below for the steps to be followed.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/fiori-tile-creation-for-custom-transaction-codes-reports/ba-p/13558745

You can refer this.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hi everyone, myself Vignesh Avasarala from Chennai, Tamilnadu, India. Happy to be joining the SAP Community! 😀💟

entry.title: Re: ABAP AUTHORITY CHECK Explained: A Practical Guide with Real-World Example
entry.content:
Hi Community,

 

  • Object class: it is a container for authorization objects  
  • Authorization object: object to check authorization 
  • Authorization field: an authorization object has the authorization field which is used to define field values 



Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi everyone, myself Vignesh Avasarala from Chennai, Tamilnadu, India. Happy to be joining the SAP Community! 😀♥️

entry.title: Re: How to Update & COMMIT in the Action without using 'c...
entry.content:
If it is a new App then the Managed application is recommended without CUD.

You can create an action and create RFC wrapper FM to put code for sending email and PO update FM like BAPI. You can use COMMIT WORK inside that.

Call the FM with DESTINATION 'NONE' which will not break anything in RAP framework.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hi everyone, myself Vignesh Avasarala from Chennai, Tamilnadu, India. Happy to be joining the SAP Community! 😀♥️

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Internal (Customs) Compliance Programs – The What, Why and How
entry.content:
What are they?

 

Let’s start with the basics: Internal (Customs) Compliance Programs are a set of internal policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with export control regulations. There is no blueprint, so the system should be tailored to each company.

 

Why should companies care?

 

Internal (Customs) Compliance Programs are recommended by the European Union, USA, China, Japan, India, Australia & many more other countries to address risks such as:

Some EU Member States even require exporter to have an Internal Export Compliance Programs in place before they will issue a global exporter license. In Japan, the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry publishes the companies that have registered their programs.

But, in addition to help mitigate risks, there are also additional advantages to having these programs in place.

These advantages can be split into 2 main categories:

  • Macro benefits at a higher – global – level sort of say, that help build a more sustainable world:

 

  • Micro benefits – that directly profits the company itself:
    • Mapping an efficient export flow
    • Obtaining knowledge and control of the end user’s nature and activities
    • Complying with export control legislation
    • Reducing the risk of penalties by having risk controls in play
    • Minimising the risk of involvement in unauthorised exports and/or proliferation practices
    • Avoiding being entered on a list of targeted entities by some bodies and authorities
    • Raising the awareness of members of staff
    • Protect corporate reputation
    • Obtain a global export authorisation

 

What does the program look like?

 

Since a picture is worth a thousand words (and in this case maybe a few more!), I thought it might be easier to simply depict a summary of the various program areas:

 

Now, for the main question: how to implement it?

 

In today’s short blog, I wanted to invite an expert in the domain to offer their insights on best practices in terms of Internal Compliance Programs. I was very fortunate that Kevin Shakespeare – Director Professional Practices at the Chartered Institute of Export & International Trade agreed share his expertise on the matter.

 

What is the Chartered Institute of Export & International Trade?

 

The Chartered Institute of Export & International Trade’s purpose is to empower organisations and equip individuals with the expertise to trade effectively, sustainably and competitively.

Established in 1935, the Chartered Institute is trusted by governments, businesses and trade professionals around the world as the leading experts in international trade and the foremost association of exporters and importers.

It is committed to professionalising trade through education and training, while partnering with national and international partners to facilitate the acknowledged mutual benefits of free, frictionless and legitimate trade.

 

From your experience, where would you recommend companies start, and progress to implement an effective program?

 

Starting the process:

The process should start with the following actions and internal reviews

  • Know your customer and know your product
  • Understanding end use / end user, and understanding risk of diversion
  • Understanding where companies in the supply chain are established
  • Carry out a gap analysis and risk analysis
  • Ensure the company has an understanding of controlled goods
  • Ensure the company know the regulations that apply to their products and services and the countries that they trade with

All internal stakeholders should be involved in the planning process and companies should ensure that all team members are qualified and trained to a sufficient level of competence. 

 

Practical implementation and understanding

The company should understand the following and ensure that procedures are clearly documented and applied.

  • Denied party screening and sanctions compliance
  • Trans-shipment licenses
  • Understand the receiving countries licensing rules
  • Post movements requirements (OGEL)
  • Clear document audit trail
  • Creating and maintaining Export controls / customs compliance policies
  • Sub-contractor awareness / supply chain visibility
  • Extra territorial jurisdictions
  • Classification for customs and export controls
  • Clear internal audit and reporting (voluntary disclosures) process
  • Digital supply chain checks where possible
  • Objectives on what the Internal Compliance programme should achieve and how the standards will be maintained

 

Security requirements

The company should ensure the following security requirements are in place and ensure that procedures are clearly document and applied.

  • Security policies for employees
  • Secure software and cloud storage locations
  • Sufficient security clearance for employees

 

How can the Chartered Institute of Export & International Trade help companies achieve their objectives in this area?

 

There are a number of ways in which the Chartered Institute of Export and International Trade can provide support, for example:

  • Company health checks on existing internal compliance progress, aligned with an analysis of existing processes  
  • Advisory services including assistance in developing, implementing, and delivering the internal programs necessary for an Internal Compliance Programme
  • Support for classifying your goods
  • Knowledge sharing
  • Regular information bulletins
  • Provision of a Regulatory calendar
  • Training programmes; public training courses and courses specific to your company
  • Applications for export licenses and customs authorisations
  • Professional and Regulated Qualifications
  • Professional member communities in Export Controls and in Customs, including special interest groups and networking opportunities
  • Authorised Economic Operator status

In the (translated) words of Lao Tzu: “Anticipate the difficult by managing the easy”. So, should you want to anticipate and stay ahead in the ever-changing world of international trade and keep up with the latest trade news and announcements, I would suggest easilly subscribing to the Chartered Institute of Export & International Trade updates!

What about you, is your company looking into Internal Compliance Programs? I look forward to reading your thoughts and comments either on this blog or on Twitter @TFrenehard

entry.title: Re: SFTP adapter - how to handle errors within processing a file
entry.content:
Hi @jirifridrich ,

Nice idea for handling retries, but what about using JMS Queue instead of custom design.

entry.title: Missing Table Analysis in DVM Reports
entry.content:
Our weekly DVM Reports are generating automatically with no issues, but a new table analysis I created is not included. The Age of Records determination for DBTABLOG (for sid E01) shows a green job status in Table Analysis Administration, but is absent from the DVM reports. What am I missing to get this new analysis included?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing Table Analysis in DVM Reports
entry.content:
Hi *,

It is normal behavior, as Table Analysis Administration and DVM Session (DVM reports) are 2 different functions.
-->"Auto DVM Session", which can analyze some pre-defined tables, and you can also try to analyze a table separately by manually creating a DVM session in SM_WORKCENTER -> Data Volume Management -> Service Sessions.

-->"Table Analysis Administration", which is commonly used for "Data Allocation Statistics" in SM_WORKCENTER -> Data Volume Management.

You can refer to the portal guide of Data Volume Management for "Table Analysis Administration"

Table Analysis Administration - Data Volume Management | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

entry.title: #CoE#LA. Direct transfer data from SAP ECC source system using communication arrangement to connect?
entry.content: How to direct transfer data from SAP ECC source system using a communication arrangement to connect? Is there any step by step guidance?

entry.title: Re: DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB SAPLCKMS...
entry.content:
To resolve the DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR during CKMLCP posting, consider the following steps:

  1. Check SAP Notes: Review the following SAP Notes specifically related to this issue:

    • 2473645
    • 2772534
    • 2682969 [1]

  2. Analyze the error: The error occurs during COMMIT WORK processing when trying to insert a record into the CKMLPP table that already exists

  3. Possible solutions:

    • Ensure that the Open SQL array insert is only used when you are certain no duplicate records exist in the database
    • Implement exception handling for CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB during COMMIT WORK processing
    • If the error occurs in an unmodified SAP program, search the SAP Notes system using keywords: "DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR", "CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB", "SAPLCKMS", "LCKMSU33", "CKMS_BUFFER_POST_DYN"

  4. Prevent SQL Injection:

    • Use static open SQL statements where possible
    • If dynamic SQL is necessary, use class CL_ABAP_DYN_PRG to implement input checks and escape the input for dynamic clauses [2]

  5. Consult SAP administrator: If the issue persists, involve your SAP administrator for further investigation and potential system-specific solutions

Remember to document the actions and entries that caused the error to aid in troubleshooting and prevention of future occurrences.

entry.title: Re: Entry is not a valid supply chain unit (/SCWM/SHP_RCV)
entry.content:
Hi, 

The issue can come due to incorrect location master data in EWM system. Kindly check the note 2735601 - Entry is not a valid supply chain unit (/SCWM/SHP_RCV 058) - SAP for Me for details. 

Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: SAP DBTech JDBC: [383] (at 38): invalid identifier: parameterized sql view USR_ATTR_ID without i
entry.content: Please give us information about what you're trying to do. How are you connecting? Which query are you running? Which client are you using?

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Mohn,

Sorry I didn't make all clear in the last reply. Query Browser app is only to preview queries created in Custom Analytical Queries app (CDS view name starts with YY1, or ZZ1), therefore queries created in ADT will not show up there. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/3a24b854ee8f8d21e10000000a44176d.html?locale=en-US

So the frontend to directly consume projection view created in ADT on S4 2023 is AO or SAC. 

At the same time, to access projection view query in SAC or AO, the better (recommended) way to setup authorization is to use S_START Authorization for INA1 Service, see  https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwplaolap/3956877110.html?locale=en-US

With this, the query don't need to set C1 released any more. The authorized queries in INA1 service will listed in AO or SAC open query box.

 

Best regards,

Giselle

entry.title: Re: India Localization Compliance Reports in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Sahil_Grover Thank you for sharing this article.

Regards,

Vignesh Avasarala

Presales Consultant

entry.title: Re: 403 forbidden error for XSUAA protected capm destinat...
entry.content:
Change your package.json to

"cds": {
  "requires": {
    "auth": {
      "kind": "jwt" //use jwt or xsuaa as value
    }
  }
}

See https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/authentication#strategies

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Solman Application for SAP Satellite...
entry.content: Dear Vera Jiang,
